title: Avoid the Use of Magic Numbers
description: Magic numbers add complexity to the code by requiring the reader to have knowledge about the global context to understand the number itself. This can make the code more difficult to understand and maintain. This rule encourages the use of named constants instead of magic numbers for clarity and maintainability. Note that -1, 0, and 1 are not considered magic numbers, and this rule also ignores hashCode methods.
recommendation: Replace magic numbers with named constants.
impact: Improves code readability and maintainability by avoiding the use of magic numbers that can be confusing or misleading.

checks.message: Replace magic numbers with named constants.
